api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/o.purpose: Auto-generated operation classes implementing every OpenMeter API endpoint as typed Python methods grouped by resource (CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations, BillingProfilesOperations, etc.). Each class uses the corehttp pipeline to build, send, and deserialize HTTP requests. Do not edit _operations.py — only _patch.py is safe to modify.

patterns.name: build_*_request free functions for request construction
patterns.description: Every operation is preceded by a build_<resource>_<verb>_request(...) free function that constructs the HttpRequest (URL path formatting, query params, headers) using the module-level _SERIALIZER singleton. The method calls this builder then sends via self._client.send_request. Never build HttpRequest objects manually outside this pattern.
patterns.example: def build_customers_list_request(*, page=None, page_size=None, **kwargs) -> HttpRequest: _params['page'] = _SERIALIZER.query('page', page, 'int'); return HttpRequest(method='GET', url='/api/v1/customers', params=_params, headers=_headers)

patterns.name: ClsType optional callback for result interception
patterns.description: Every operation method accepts an optional cls: ClsType kwarg — a callback(pipeline_response, deserialized, headers) that can intercept or transform the result. Pass None (the default) for standard use. Never remove this parameter.
patterns.example: ClsType = Optional[Callable[[PipelineResponse[HttpRequest, HttpResponse], T, dict[str, Any]], Any]]

patterns.name: map_error for typed HTTP error mapping before deserialization
patterns.description: Operations call map_error(status_code=..., response=..., error_map={404: ResourceNotFoundError, 409: ResourceExistsError, ...}) before deserializing success responses. Never catch HttpResponseError without calling map_error first.

patterns.name: Module-level _SERIALIZER singleton — do not reinstantiate
patterns.description: _SERIALIZER = Serializer() is a module-level singleton with client_side_validation=False. Never create per-call Serializer() instances. All query/header/URL serialization goes through _SERIALIZER.

patterns.name: ItemPaged for paginated list operations
patterns.description: List operations that return paginated collections return ItemPaged[T] from corehttp.paging. Callers iterate the result directly; the pager handles next-page fetching transparently.
patterns.example: result: ItemPaged[Customer] = ops.list(page=1, page_size=20); for customer in result: print(customer.name)

key_file_guides.role: All ~30 generated operation classes, each initialized with a PipelineClient and OpenMeterClientConfiguration. Contains all build_*_request helpers and the send+deserialize logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by make gen-api. _SERIALIZER is module-level; do not create per-request instances.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all Operations classes and applies _patch.py via _patch_sdk(). The __all__ list is authoritative.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. Any symbols added in _patch.py must appear in _patch.__all__ to be included in __all__.

key_file_guides.role: The only file safe to hand-edit. Override or extend generated operation class methods here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain callable. Methods added here must not conflict with generated signatures.

anti_patterns: Editing _operations.py directly — overwritten by make gen-api
Building HttpRequest objects manually outside the build_*_request free-function pattern — breaks serialization consistency
Catching HttpResponseError without calling map_error first — loses typed exception mapping (ResourceNotFoundError, ResourceExistsError, etc.)
Creating per-request Serializer() instances instead of using the module-level _SERIALIZER singleton
Removing the cls: ClsType parameter from any operation method signature

decisions.decision: Request construction split into build_*_request free functions separate from send+deserialize logic in Operations methods
decisions.rationale: Enables unit-testing request construction (URL, headers, params) without an HTTP client, and keeps the send path uniform across all operations.

decisions.decision: Operations classes grouped by resource (CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations, etc.) rather than by HTTP method
decisions.rationale: Resource-grouped classes match the TypeSpec interface groupings and make it easy to find all operations for a domain without scanning the entire file.

code_examples.scenario: Invoke a paginated list operation and a single-resource get through the operations classes
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated.operations import CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations

# ops is injected by the top-level OpenMeterClient; not constructed directly
for customer in ops.customers.list(page=1, page_size=20):
    print(customer.name)

invoice = ops.invoices.get(invoice_id='01HXYZ...')

api/client/python/openmeter/aio/operatio.purpose: Public async operations namespace shim that re-exports all async operation classes from the auto-generated layer (_generated/aio/operations) into the stable openmeter.aio.operations namespace, applying optional monkey-patch overrides via _patch.py. No business logic — a thin re-export boundary.

patterns.name: Wildcard re-export from generated layer
patterns.description: All operations are imported from ..._generated.aio.operations._operations via wildcard import. Never define new operation classes directly in __init__.py.

patterns.name: Patch overlay applied after base import
patterns.description: _patch.py overrides are imported after _operations so patch symbols take precedence. patch_sdk() must be called at module end to activate runtime patches.

patterns.name: __all__ extension pattern
patterns.description: __all__ is set to the generated layer's __all__, then extended with patch symbols not already present. Generated names first, patch additions appended.
patterns.example: __all__ = __all__; __all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

patterns.name: TYPE_CHECKING-only patch import guard
patterns.description: The _patch wildcard import is guarded by TYPE_CHECKING for static analysis, then repeated unconditionally for runtime. This double-import pattern is intentional Azure SDK convention.

patterns.name: Graceful ImportError fallback for patch
patterns.description: The _patch import is wrapped in try/except ImportError so the SDK works normally when no patch file exists.
patterns.example:
try: from ..._generated.aio.operations._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
except ImportError: _patch_all = []

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; the public re-export shim. Importing from openmeter.aio.operations resolves here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add new symbols or logic. New async operations go in _generated/aio/operations/_operations.py (generated) or _patch.py (override). Changing import order breaks patch precedence. Removing _patch_sdk() silently disables runtime patches.

anti_patterns: Defining new operation classes or methods directly in __init__.py — all operations come from _generated
Removing the _patch_sdk() call at module end — runtime patches silently do not apply
Manually editing _generated/aio/operations/_operations.py — auto-generated and overwritten on next SDK generation
Importing specific names instead of wildcard — breaks compatibility when _generated adds new operations
Reordering base import and patch import — patch import must come after _operations import to override base

decisions.decision: Wildcard re-export shim instead of direct _generated imports
decisions.rationale: Gives consumers a stable openmeter.aio.operations namespace that survives generator churn; the _generated path is an internal implementation detail consumers should not depend on.

decisions.decision: Optional _patch.py layer with graceful ImportError handling
decisions.rationale: Allows SDK customisations (auth, retry, helpers) to be injected without forking the generator; if no patch file exists the SDK still works normally.

code_examples.scenario: Correct structure of __init__.py — re-export with patch overlay
code_examples.code:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from ..._generated.aio.operations._patch import *

from ..._generated.aio.operations._operations import *  # noqa: F401, F403

try:
    from ..._generated.aio.operations._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
    from ..._generated.aio.operations._patch import *  # noqa: F401, F403
except ImportError:
    _patch_all = []
from ..._generated.aio.operations._patch import patch_sdk as _patch_sdk

from ..._generated.aio.operations import __all__
__all__ = __all__
__all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])
_patch_sdk()

api/client/python/openmeter/models.purpose: Public re-export facade for all generated Python SDK model types (dataclasses and enums). Delegates entirely to _generated/models — no hand-written model code; provides a stable import path for consumers regardless of generator internals.

patterns.name: Wildcard re-export from generated layer
patterns.description: Re-export _models and _enums via wildcard imports from _generated.models. Never define or duplicate types in this package.
patterns.example:
from .._generated.models._models import *  # noqa: F401, F403
from .._generated.models._enums import *  # noqa: F401, F403

patterns.name: Optional _patch overlay
patterns.description: Try-import _generated.models._patch and merge its __all__ into the module __all__. Patch symbols extend (never replace) the generated set.
patterns.example:
try: from .._generated.models._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
except ImportError: _patch_all = []

patterns.name: TYPE_CHECKING guard for patch stubs
patterns.description: Import _patch under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` for IDE type-narrowing without runtime cost.

patterns.name: Call _patch_sdk() at module load
patterns.description: Always call _patch_sdk() after all re-exports to apply monkey-patches from the generated layer.

patterns.name: __all__ propagation
patterns.description: Derive __all__ from the generated module, then extend with patch-only symbols for a complete, non-redundant export list.
patterns.example: __all__ = __all__; __all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; thin facade merging generated models + patch overrides into one importable namespace (openmeter.models.*).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add hand-written model classes here — clobbered on the next make gen-api. Do not import deeper than _models/_enums/_patch from _generated internals.

anti_patterns: Defining or modifying model classes directly in this package instead of using the _patch mechanism
Removing the _patch_sdk() call — patch hooks silently do not apply
Manually maintaining __all__ instead of deriving it from the generated module
Duplicating generated types here — they drift immediately on next codegen run

decisions.decision: Thin facade over generated code rather than duplicating types
decisions.rationale: All model types are generated from TypeSpec via make gen-api; any hand-written copy would drift immediately. The facade gives a stable import path (openmeter.models.*) regardless of generator internals.

code_examples.scenario: Full __init__.py structure — the only valid pattern for this package
code_examples.code:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .._generated.models._patch import *

from .._generated.models._models import *  # noqa: F401, F403
from .._generated.models._enums import *  # noqa: F401, F403

try:
    from .._generated.models._patch import __all__ as _patch_all
    from .._generated.models._patch import *  # noqa: F401, F403
except ImportError:
    _patch_all = []
from .._generated.models._patch import patch_sdk as _patch_sdk

from .._generated.models import __all__
__all__ = __all__
__all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])
_patch_sdk()
